classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods.
The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.

metadata.author: The Canons of the Councils of Ancyra, Gangra, Neocæsarea, Antioch and Laodicea, which Canons were Accepted and Received by the Ecumenical Synods

title: Canon XVI.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: If, under any pretence of piety, any children shall forsake their parents, particularly [if the parents are] believers, and shall withhold becoming reverence from their parents, on the plea that they honour piety more than them, let them be anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: If children leave their parents who are of the faithful let them be anathema.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Zonaras notes that the use of the word “particularly” shews that the obligation is universal.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: The commentators all refer here to St. Matthew xv., where our Lord speaks of the subterfuge by which the Jews under pretext of piety defrauded their parents and made the law of God of none effect.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: Of the last clause this is the meaning; that according to the Eustathians “piety towards God” or “divine worship,” or rather its pretence, should be preferred to the honour and reverence due to parents.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: This canon, in Isidore’s version, is found in the Corpus Juris Canonici, Gratian’s Decretum, Pars I., Dist. xxx., c. i.

verses.book: The Canons of the Holy Fathers Assembled at Gangra, Which Were Set Forth After the Council of Nice.
verses.text: The Roman correctors advertize the reader that the version of Dionysius Exiguus “is much nearer to the original Greek, although not altogether so.”
